seoTitle: Davis Creek vs Dawes Point — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Davis Creek and Dawes Point. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Davis Creek vs Dawes Point: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Davis Creek and Dawes Point.
heading: Davis Creek vs Dawes Point — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Davis Creek, New South Wales and Dawes Point, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Davis Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Dawes Point (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Davis Creek or Dawes Point safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Davis Creek has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Dawes Point scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Davis Creek scores 31/100 for bushfire risk. Dawes Point scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Davis Creek or Dawes Point?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Davis Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
